entry.title: Consuming complex Datasphere models
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Datasphere is a part of the new SAP Business Data Cloud offering, which enables a business data fabric architecture that uniquely harmonizes mission-critical data across the organization.

One prime feature of Datasphere is the semantically rich data model, that allows for deep structures of dimensions, hierarchies and transactional data. 
The data models created in Datasphere can then be consumed for reporting by various tools, both SAP and non-SAP tools and used as the basis of reporting.

And while the "Single source of truth" is the cornerstone of enterprise reporting, the individual tools may interpret the data models differently. 

 

Purpose

My main objective in this blog is to show how different tools interprets an Analytical Model and what you may want to consider when building models. 

I will focus on the Analytical Model, rather than consumption models / perspective in this blog, because the end-result is the same, and the Analytical Model is the object that most people will first try to consume for reporting. 

 

Basic concepts

Before we dive in, I want to outline a few basic concepts that is important to understand the mechanisms at play. If you are already familiar with the concepts, you can skip this section. 

Annotations

With the introduction of CDS views, SAP introduced Annotations, which is a tool to semantically enri

ch a table or view with instructions for the analytical engine to interpret the data. 
While there are many, many different types of annotations, Datasphere primarily uses Consumption and Semantics. 

Take the below, very simple table

MaterialQuantitySales Unit
MAT1100KG
MAT250TON

We can add the following annotations to the table:

ColumnCDS Annotation
Materialnone
Quantity@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Sales Unit'
Sales Unit@Semantics.unitOfMeasure: true

Which does nothing to the table definition itself, but we've told the engine that 'Quantity' and 'Unit' belong together.

Associations

One of the, at least to me, best things that came with CDS views was 'associations'. 

In short, an association between two tables allows the engine to fine-tune the query, based on what you need, without you having to join tables together. 

Based on the associations and annotations, the analytical engine can dynamically navigate the underlaying objects to fit the demands of the user at runtime. 

You can read more about associations in the SAP documentation or one of the many blog posts. 

Semantic usage

When modelling objects in Datasphere, you will have the option to mark a table or view with a semantic usage, which provides some higher level annotations to the object. 

Datasphere allows for the following options 

  • Fact - Contains one or more measures and attributes. A fact typically has associations pointing to one or more dimensions and is consumed by analytic models (see Create a Fact to Contain Measurable Data).
  • Dimension - Contains attributes containing master data like a product list or store directory, and supporting hierarchies (see Create a Dimension to Categorize Data).
  • Hierarchy - Contains attributes defining a parent-child hierarchy (see Create an External Hierarchy for Drill-Down).
  • Hierarchy with Directory - Contains one or more parent-child hierarchies (see Create a Hierarchy with Directory).
  • Text - Contains attributes used to provide textual content in one or more languages (see Create a Text Entity for Attribute Translation).
  • Relational Dataset - [default] Contains columns with no specific analytical purpose.

 

Creating a complex data model

When I use the word "complex", I don't mean big but deep. A data model can have a hundred dimensions, and still be pretty simple.

In this example, I've created a straight forward data model, which the following objects:

DescriptionSemantic Usage

Employee

Fact

Department

Dimension

Education

Dimension

Job Function

Dimension

Job Level

Dimension

The main object in this data model is the employee, which has several attributes: (1) They belong to a department, have an (2) education, and a (3) job function. 
In addition, each job function has another property: (4) job level, which indicates if it is a management position. 

You can see the relationship in a graphical representation here:

When I say "complex" data model, this is what I mean: Some dimensions have no direct link to the main object - as you might have in a star schema - but can still be consumed. 

In a real world scenario, you can imagine an almost infinite amount of levels and data links. 

Imagine, for instance that we extended the "Education" dimension with the following extra fields: (1) Level (Bachelor, master, PhD, ect.) which also needs it one text, (2) area of study (such as engineering, finance, accounting, etc.), (3) completion date, (4) school of graduation, which may be a whole other dimension all by itself. 

You can imagine how quickly we can add layers to a data model, which might technically be "correct" in a data modelling sense, but can present other problems, as we can see shortly. 

When we create our Analytical Model, we have the option to select the associations we want from our "Fact" object.

Take note of a few things here:

  • Some of the fields are marked with a cube, which marks them as a Dimension (See the red squares).
  • All the dimensions have a "T" on their line, meaning that the dimension contains both key and text
  • Notice the "Date" dimension has a little stair / hierarchy icon as well, meaning that the association also comes with a hierarchy.

As you saw above, our "Job Function" dimension have attributes of it's own, so we can choose that and see that it has two attributes, but only one of them is a dimension:

The final analytical model looks like this:

 

Consuming the Analytical Model

As promised, we shall now see how the Analytical model can be consumed with all the semantic enrichment. I will go through the following tools:

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud via a Live Connection
  2. Microsoft Excel via an SAP Add-In
  3. 3rd Party tools via OData

Consumption in general

I have skipped over a lot of different topics, so I would absolutely recommend the official documentation on consumption to see in more detail. 

SAP Analytics Cloud via a Live Connection

To be able to use this connection, you must have a user in both Datasphere and SAC, and have a working live connection (see Live Data Connections to SAP Datasphere in the SAP Analytics Cloud documentation). 

When you create a story in SAC, or just edit an existing one, you have the option to add a data source:

From here, you can see a subfolder called Datasphere. Select the correct Datasphere and Space:

And select your Model:

Choose Analytical Model

You can now consume the model including all associations and metadata.
While there are a lot to explore, consider these two points:

  1. The attribute “Management” is available as a property under Job Function and can be used in modelling
  2. While the actual data only contains 1, 2, 3 in the “Department” column, because we have created the association to texts in Datasphere, we can use the texts with no extra work.

Additionally, take note of the "Date" column, where we have both attributes and the hierarchy:

Which is absolutely fantastic! 
No objects are transferred to SAC, it just acts as a tunnel between Datasphere and the end-user. 

However, there is one problem. As you may have noticed, Job Level is not visible as a property of "Job Function", whereas "Management" is. "Job Level" appears as it's own dimension.

SAC displays nested dimensions as their own entity, rather than related to "Job Function". Which may be perfectly alright, but might cause confusion for the people building the reports, that might not know the underlaying data model. 
Imagine you have some sales data with a ship-to and ship-from columns. Both of these might have an "Address" dimension, and may cause confusion. 

Microsoft Excel via an SAP Add-In

The SAP Analytics Cloud add-in (which is not the same as Analysis for Office), can simply be downloaded from the Microsoft Store. 

After installing, you can add the model to Excel, very similar to SAC:

And will interpret the metadata in the exact same way SAC does:

3rd Party tools via an OData Service

Non-SAP products, such as Power BI or Tableau, can consume the data from Datasphere through an OData service.

Setup

You can find other guides to help you set up a connection for your service of choice, but the setup in Datasphere is the same for all tools. 
I highly recommend @gustavokath blogpost on the topic, which has all the information you need to set up your client.

The OData request

Datasphere allows you to build and preview an OData request from the Analytical Model builder:

Which allows you to play and and configure the request and will generate a URL for you:

If you preview the data, you can see what kind of response it will generate. 
However, there are a lot of things to note here, so let's take them one at a time.

1. Datasphere completely flattens the data before it sends it: 

The dimensions are served as both the key, and the text. 
Note that the "Dimension" is served as the technical name of the dimension.
All Text fields added this way will have the suffix "_T", but uses the technical name of the field in the annotation:

Which can make the output difficult to read. 

2. All data is served as either string or number (decimal, integer ect).

This may not seem as such a big deal, until you consider the dates:

And while it can seem like a non-issue, the data itself contains no hint as to the format. 
You can request the metadata and see the type of the field is Edm.Date, which is a nice standard format:

But that only happens because we've specified the column as a Date:

But you can imagine different sources serving data in different formats, which can make consumption and modelling a nightmare, unless you enforce strict data harmonization in Datasphere - which you should obviously do - but mistakes happen. 

3. Nested dimensions are flattened with the business name, not technical name.

There is, however, one absolutely brain-melting thing that I personally foresee causing endless amounts of confusion. 

The nested dimension M_LEVEL, seen above as "Job Level" is a part of the data, but it is represented by the description of the parent dimension:

Just looking at this output, you have a ice cube's chance in hell to deduce which fields belong together. 
Again, you can query the metadata and see the relationship, but you know as well as I do, that at some point, the data is going to end up in the hands of a super-user who has a very specific use case, and will be absolutely floored. 

And yes - you are correct. If I had named my dimensions and fields better, a lot of the confusion could be avoided. Absolutely. That is kind of the point 🙂 

 

Conclusion

The ability to consume Analytical Models build in Datasphere is an absolute godsend. 
It allows you to build central complex, semantically rich data model and allow your users to build their own reports, with whatever tools - to an extent - they prefer. 

However, you as the data engineer have a responsibility to find the sweet spot between a model that contains 'enough', without it becoming too complicated. 

Sometimes, two models might be better than one.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter May 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Explore SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI

Join our remote workshop to explore Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors HCM. This Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) offers hands-on experience and expert support. Learn AI features, validate requirements, set up use cases, and more. Sign up now and enhance your skills with SAP’s latest innovations.

Sign up

Guidance for a Successful go-live of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Ensure a successful go-live with the new CQC deployment readiness check service for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. The service is provided through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council program, designed to ensure a smooth, secure, and stable deployment experience for your digital workplace transformation. Availability is limited, so make sure to secure your slot.

Full details

Quality and Stability in SAP Field Service Management

The continuous quality check for deployment readiness for SAP Field Service Management is a remote service–included with your SAP Cloud subscription. It helps ensure operational efficiency and readiness by performing a detailed analysis of your FSM solution, either before go-live or as a "health check" for your productive systems.

Read On

SAP Business Data Cloud: Expert-guided Implementation Series

We are delighted to bring you our series of Expert-guided Implementation workshops on SAP Business Data Fabric including a brand-new session dedicated to SAP Business Data Cloud.

Secure your seat and sign up today

Another Avenue for Enhanced Project Support and Resources

Join SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council and gain exclusive project support, early access to advanced tools, and expert guidance - all while contributing your feedback to enhance our services.


New workstreams for Q2, 2025 focus on

  • Going Live Support for SAP Cloud Integration
  • Deployment Readiness for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP Tactical Supply Planning (Response and Supply or Sales and Operations)
  • Deployment Readiness for SAP Sales and Service Cloud, Version 2

Learn more & register

Successfully go-live with the SAP Cloud Integration Capability

The CQC (continuous quality check) support service for SAP Cloud Integration is designed to monitor the performance and resource consumption of the SAP Cloud Integration tenant during the go-live. Key performance indicators are analyzed to detect critical situations.

Learn how the service helps your go live and register here

Discover the Future of Transformation, AI, and Foundational Support at SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando

Discover how SAP is transforming support and business operations with SAP Enterprise Support, AI, SAP Cloud ALM, clean core strategy and smooth customer onboarding! From agentic AI to an integrated toolchain and real-world customer insights with customers like Carhartt, SGWS, Panasonic and Nissan, our sessions at SAP Sapphire Orlando (May 19–21) are packed with innovation and inspiration.

Explore the sessions and get inspired here

Unlock the Power of Cloud, AI, and Business Transformation at SAP Sapphire 2025 in Madrid

Ready to shape your clean core journey and boost system resilience with AI? Join us in Madrid (May 26–28) to hear from SAP experts, customers like Bosch and Douglas, and discover how SAP Enterprise Support, SAP Cloud ALM, the integrated toolchain, Rise and GROW with SAP, and onboarding tools are driving smarter transformations.

Get all the details and plan your visit here

Discover SAP Business AI in Spend Management and SAP Business Network

Curious about how SAP Business AI is enhancing spend management and the SAP Business Network? We've curated essential resources for you in one easy-to-access spot! Explore the new SAP Business AI in Spend Management and SAP Business Network page—available through the corresponding Value Map. Discover how AI is driving smarter processes, inclusivity, and a future-ready approach to spend management.

Visit page [value map access required]

Live Sessions on SAP Enterprise Support – Expert-guided Implementation (EGI)

Join the upcoming session where our value map experts will explain the Expert-guided implementation (EGI) delivery format from SAP Enterprise Support, focusing on SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI and SAP Business Data Cloud. These multi-day remote workshops are part of our foundational support offering and are included with your SAP cloud subscription. Attend to ask questions live and learn more about EGIs.

Save May 14 and register here

O Captain! My Captain! Agentic AI Leads the Way in the Digital Age

Words and ideas can change the world, and agentic AI is the captain leading this transformation. Free up your mind, but there’s no need to rely on imagination alone: this SAP Community article shares the key features and details of agentic AI that help in the decision-making process and the automation of tasks.

Read the full story

SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise

Start with our enhanced e-learning to get an introduction to SAP S/4HANA for advanced available-to-promise. Get upskilled on topics such as product availability check, backorder processing, business process scheduling, product allocation check, and many more.

Join the E-learning

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Mee-the-Expert live session on May 13, June 20 that helps you get on board.

Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. We have two upcoming sessions in May (starting May 20, and May 26).

More in this blog post

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday Expert-guided Implementation training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. Mark your calendar for May 12 and register.

More in this  Blog Post

Enhance Your SAP Field Service Management Experience with Continuous Quality Checks

Are you implementing SAP Field Service Management (FSM) or managing its day-to-day operations? In both scenarios, ensuring the quality and stability of your solution is vital. SAP offers continuous quality checks (CQCs) help you achieve this. 

Check out the following blog post to see which CQCs are available to support your FSM solution, tailored to your specific implementation phase and needs.

Full blog

Join our Biweekly Open Office Hours: SAP Cloud Identity Services

Identity Authentication service is the go-forward approach across many SAP solutions, and a prerequisite for Joule. We host regular Office Hours for SAP customers and partners to get SAP expert support and/or advice.

Register for our regular Office Hours for the following SAP solutions:

SAP SuccessFactors HCM sessions register here
Ariba and Fieldglass sessions register here

New Customer Insights Features Within SAP for Me

SAP for Me  “Customer Insights” has launched exciting updates in the Customer Insights dashboard, designed to enhance usage insights, partner interactions, and onboarding processes for businesses. Dive into these new features to optimize performance and gain a competitive edge.

Functional Usage Insights: The new "Business Value" added to the Functional Usage section allows businesses to see the impact of their functional usage on business value and compare their usage with industry peers. This insight helps identify opportunities for adoption and improvement by comparing customer usage to the industry peers regarding usage intensity and overall usage. View more here.

Partner Case Analysis: With the extended information in the Cases section, customers can now accurately analyse cases created by partners. This feature aids in evaluating different aspects like volume, quality, and priority of partner interactions with SAP Support, ensuring transparency and high-standard service outsourcing.

Enhanced Onboarding Visualization: The newly released Onboarding section uses SAP EarlyWatch Alert reports to fully visualize the onboarding status for customer systems for SAP IBP. The detailed graphs show mandatory milestones and the duration each system stays in a phase, marking overdue milestones to prompt necessary actions. For a deeper exploration of these features, visit the "Customer Insights" in SAP for Me and check out the accompanying blogs for each section. View more here.

AI + Automation = 100% Uptime! Discover SAP’s Holiday Readiness Secrets

Join Oliver Huschke in this episode of SAP Change Makers as he speaks with Tarun Luthra, Head of Technical Support and Global VP for SAP Industries and Customer Experience. Together, they explore how AI and automation are transforming peak season resilience, ensuring 100% uptime during Black Friday and Cyber Monday. Learn how SAP’s Holiday Support Readiness Program leverages Cure360 and SmartResolve to prevent system issues before they happen—keeping businesses running and billions in GMV secured. If you want to know how AI-driven support is reshaping digital commerce, this is an episode you can’t miss!

Link to podcast

To Infinity and Beyond: AI Expands Shopping Experience with a World of Possibilities

The set of AI possibilities seems to grow in the speed of a light-year, and we wouldn’t want to miss it for the world. Check this SAP Community Article to see what they can offer to improve shopping experiences for customers during peak seasons

See here

Learn More

  • Subscribe here for the newsletter
  • Guidance on signing up
  • ES Academy - All you need to know
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps
  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: 2 simple questions about SAC table
entry.content:
Hi,

Here are two simple questions we want to figure out:

1. Is it possible to sort by 2 measures on a single table?

2. How to filter the rows where the corresponding measure value > 25%?

Thanks for your help.

Regards,

Jason

entry.title: Sapphire Orlando&Madrid 2025: World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, My name is Joschua Goetz and I'm an IT Technology Services Senior Specialist, working in a very special project about esports. Together with our Partner Team Liquid we build esports analytics dashboards to boost team and player performance.

I’m excited to participate at SAP Sapphire Orlando and Madrid this year, where I’ll be joined by Team Liquid to talk about our awesome partnership and how we use analytics to help them win.

My Sessions Overview

Orlando:
World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software - DIS2157 

Here i have the honor to present together with Thom Valks Partnerships Manager at Team Liquid. Our session is at 3:30 PM - 3:50 PM EDT on May 20. 

Madrid:
World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software - DIS1454 

In Madrid i'll be on stage with Jason Lucas Luijckx Director of Partnerships at Team Liquid. Our session is scheduled at 11:30 AM - 11:50 AM CEST on May 28.

What you will learn about

The thrilling intersection of esports and innovative technology as you join us to experience the transformative power of SAP software in competitive gaming. Learn how our internal IT helps leading esports organization Team Liquid with SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP AI Core, Joule, and SAP Business Technology Platform.

Looking forward to seeing you

The world of esports is a data rich, high stakes world of competitive gaming. With SAP software it is possible to gain that ever so small edge over your opponents. Curious how we did it with Team Liquid ?

Register here for our SAP Sapphire sessions in Orlando or Madrid

Not able to attend SAP Sapphire but still curious ? Learn more about our partnership here SAP x Team Liquid 

Now let’s hear from you

Did you know that SAP was involved in esports ? 
If you play DOTA2 or League of Legends, what do you think could be some of the most influential parts of the game we should look at ?
Share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: Monitoring the resource consumption of seamless planning in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post is intended to provide a guide on how to measure the amount of resources that your system uses for seamless planning.

With seamless planning you are able to store the data of planning models from SAP Analytics Cloud in SAP Datasphere. Consequently, resources of SAP Datasphere are used for data storage, planning activities and analytic queries. The purpose of this blog is to guide you through the options of monitoring those resource consumption. Starting from your first planning model to the overall tenant resources you will be shown easy and straight forward methods to get the information you need.

The target personas are the central SAP Datasphere Administrator for a tenant wide perspective and key users of an implementation project, who wish to monitor the resource consumption of their project.

How to find your seamless planning model in SAP Datasphere

Let’s start at the beginning: Once you create a planning model in SAP Analytics Cloud you now have the option to store your data in SAP Datasphere. You are then asked to provide a space in SAP Datasphere as a storage location. In the background SAC Analytics Cloud is now creating a hidden HANA schema in said space.

In order to find out where your model resides in SAP Datasphere and how much storage it consumes, please go to the space monitoring (Space Management -> Select your space -> Monitor). As this monitoring view covers the entire space, it is helpful to filter for the seamless planning schema which is called <SPACE_NAME>#SAC_<some ID>.

Filtering the table list for the model ID which you find in the URL of your SAP Analytics Cloud planning model shows you exactly how the components of your model are stored: The first table in the screen shot shows the fact table which is followed by master data tables for the dimensions and text tables for member description translation. Please note that the fact table contains only published versions. The data used in private versions are stored in separate tables starting with “sap.fpa.services.epm.versions”. As the model ID is the last element of the table name, it may be truncated. Noticeably, there are no tables containing comments that are set for the planning model, as those are stored in SAP Analytics Cloud and not accessible from SAP Datasphere.

Finding your seamless planning objects in Datasphere

You now have an overview of the disk and memory used for storage of your seamless planning model and its private versions.

How to find the storage used for seamless planning in a space…

… via the Space Management

Now that we have the information we need for the first model, let’s aggregate that up to the storage per space. Luckily, the monitoring dashboard in the screenshot provides a donut and a bar chart depicting the storage used per schema in that space. As seamless planning creates one SAP HANA schema per space that uses seamless planning, all we need to do is filter for the schema containing ‘#SAC_” after the space name.

Storage used per schema in a space

… via the Database Explorer

The same information is also present in the SAP HANA monitoring views. To access those create a data base user in the space management and open the Database Explorer.

How to navigate to the Database Explorer

The views currently used to provide the record count and storage size in disk and memory are M_CS_TABLES for memory information and M_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_STATISTICS for disk information. Please note, that these monitoring views contain only information about column tables that are loaded into memory and thus the calculation might change in the future. To incorporate more scenarios, we will encapsule those calculations in a temporary view. To follow the subsequent instructions, please run the following SQL to create the temporary view used in this blog as a baseline:

create view TEMP_DSP_SAC_STORAGE as select 
SUBSTRING(m.schema_name, 1, LOCATE(m.schema_name, '#SAC_')-1) as SPACE_NAME,
m.schema_name,m.table_name,
m.record_count,
d.disk_size as Disk_Size,
(CASE
    WHEN m."LOAD_UNIT" = 'PAGE' THEN 0
    ELSE CASE
      WHEN m."LOADED" = 'FULL' THEN m."MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL"
      WHEN m."LOADED" != 'FULL' AND m."ESTIMATED_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL" = -1 AND RECORD_COUNT = 0 THEN 0
      WHEN m."LOADED" != 'FULL' AND m."ESTIMATED_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL" = -1 AND m."LAST_ESTIMATED_MEMORY_SIZE" >= 0 THEN m."LAST_ESTIMATED_MEMORY_SIZE"
      ELSE m."ESTIMATED_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL"
    END
  END) AS "USED_MEMORY"
FROM  M_CS_TABLES m left JOIN M_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_STATISTICS d on m.schema_name=d.schema_name and m.table_name=d.table_name
WHERE m.schema_name like '%#SAC_%' ;

To calculate the storage used for seamless planning within the space use the following SQL-Statement:

SELECT SPACE_NAME,SCHEMA_NAME, count(TABLE_NAME) as NO_OF_TABLES, sum(DISK_SIZE) as DISK_SIZE, sum(USED_MEMORY) as USED_MEMORY
FROM TEMP_DSP_SAC_STORAGE
WHERE SCHEMA_NAME LIKE '<YOUR_SPACE>#SAC_%'
group by SPACE_NAME,SCHEMA_NAME;

The result provides an aggregate of storage used for the seamless planning content within <YOUR_SPACE>.

How to find the storage used for seamless planning within the tenant

General information on memory consumed by the tenant is found in the system monitoring. Here, the seamless planning data is included in the “Data in Spaces” sections.

Storage used tenant wide is part of "Data in Spaces"

As the monitoring entails all the data stored in the spaces, we need to use SQL to filter out the seamless planning specific part. Therefore, we use the previously introduced temporary monitoring view to derive a list of spaces that are used for seamless planning and list their memory use:

SELECT SPACE_NAME,SCHEMA_NAME, count(TABLE_NAME) as NO_OF_TABLES, sum(DISK_SIZE) as DISK_SIZE, sum(USED_MEMORY) as USED_MEMORY
FROM TEMP_DSP_SAC_STORAGE
group by SPACE_NAME,SCHEMA_NAME
order by USED_MEMORY desc;

To aggregate the total storage consumed by all schemas used for seamless planning on the tenant, execute the following SQL statement:

SELECT sum(DISK_SIZE) as DISK_SIZE, sum(USED_MEMORY) as USED_MEMORY
FROM TEMP_DSP_SAC_STORAGE;

This concludes the resources consumed for the data seamless planning stores on Datasphere. Next, we will have a look at the compute resources required for calculation during planning activities.

 

How to find the memory and CPU used for seamless planning activities…

With seamless planning it is not only the data storage that is used in SAP Datasphere but also the computation required for planning activities like creating a new planning version, executing a data action and publishing a planning version. Therefore, this section focuses on estimating the memory and CPU usage of those planning activities. Please note that analytical requests for querying the data can also be captured in a similar way but it is not the focus of this blog.

As the seamless planning activities are created and started in SAP Analytics Cloud, they are not captured in the monitoring of Task Logs in SAP Datasphere. Therefore, we will trace them with the expensive statement tracing to estimate their resource consumption. The results can be seen in the System Monitor UI, the HANA Cockpit and the Database Explorer. We will cover all three methods.

First, enable expensive statement tracing in the system configuration.

How to enable expensive statement tracing

Set a threshold that is high enough to filter the ‘background noise’, and low enough that the statement you expect to be expensive is still traced. For instance, you could start with 500 milliseconds. Now all statements that run for a longer time than the set threshold will be logged and can be viewed in the System Monitor’s Statement Logs.

With the tracing active in SAP Datasphere, go to SAP Analytics Cloud and trigger the planning activity you want to evaluate.

… via the System Monitor

Next, go back to SAP Datasphere: In the System Monitor the tab “Statement Logs” is now available and your planning activity can be analyzed by the first method.

How to find seamless planning activities in the expensive statement logs

At the moment, planning activities are not clearly marked as such, so here are some indicators to identify them:

  • Check “Start Time” for the time frame when the planning activity was running
  • check “Schema Name” for your space name,
  • the “Activity” is most likely a “CALL” action.

While all those statements together make up the entire load of your activity, the main bulk of the activity is probably handled by a single statement with the highest memory usage and most probably has Statement Details that execute an MDS call.

… via the HANA Cockpit

For the second method, click on the Database Overview (HANA Cockpit) button and navigate to Performance Monitor -> Expensive Statements. There, you can see the spikes in resource consumption corresponding to expensive planning activities.

Finding planning activities in the HANA Cockpit

… via the Database Explorer

Lastly, this information and more details can also be found in the Database Explorer with the help of the monitoring view M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS:

SELECT SCHEMA_NAME, START_TIME, OBJECT_NAME,STATEMENT_STRING, DURATION_MICROSEC, MEMORY_SIZE, CPU_TIME
FROM M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS
WHERE SCHEMA_NAME LIKE '%#SAC_%' AND OPERATION = 'CALL'
ORDER BY START_TIME DESC;

Finding planning activities via the Database Explorer

Please be aware that the timestamps of the START_TIME column may not match as the database output is stored in UTC time zone and the SAP Datasphere UI displays the time zone configuration of your device.

This concludes the analysis of memory and CPU usage for planning activities such as creating a planning version (and copying data), executing a data action and publishing a planning version. While analytical queries can also be traced and found in the expensive statements sections, they do contain different statement details.

Conclusion

Following this blog post supports you in monitoring storage and computation in your system that is used for seamless planning and to judge the resources needed for it to run smoothly. For further information about sizing information for seamless planning, visit the SAP Note 3564858 - SAP Datasphere Tenant Configuration for Planning Use Cases.

entry.title: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Selecting the right analytical tool within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud framework can be pivotal to addressing your business needs effectively. Embedded analytics within SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a suite of tools to answer diverse business questions, foster data-driven decision-making, and streamline operations. Here, we’ll delve into the factors that should guide your choice in selecting the most suitable embedded analytical tool for your organization and requirements. 

  1. Tailored Support for Diverse Needs
  2. Overview on all available analytical tools inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Review Booklet
    • Multidimensional Analysis
    • Overview Page
    • Analytical List Page
    • Smart Business KPIs
    • Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story
    • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel
  3. Checklist
  4. Feature / Function / Capability focus
  5. Self-service learning

Tailored Support for Diverse Needs

The landscape of business analytics is vast, and having the correct tool set can significantly enhance business operations by:

  • Addressing specific business queries and needs.
  • Increasing user adoption and engagement with analytical tools.
  • Enabling business self-service for ongoing data insights into operations.

When navigating through SAP S/4HANA Cloud's embedded analytics offerings, consider the following factors to align with your business goals: 

  1. 🧠 Key User Availability & Expertise: Ensure that the users have the necessary skills and expertise to interact with the chosen tool.
  2. 🏢 Line of Business (LoB): Matching the tool to the specific needs of departments such as finance, sales, or procurement can lead to more insightful analysis.
  3. 📅 Report Type and Cadence: Determine whether you require mass reports, individual reports, or a mix; and establish the frequency of these reports (daily, weekly, monthly).
  4. 📊 Report Complexity: Evaluate the complexity regarding multiple KPIs and dimensions to choose a suitable tool.
  5. 🌐 Business Domain Coverage: Establish whether your needs involve one or multiple domains.

Furthermore, different personas use analytics to drive specific aspects of their roles, leveraging different types and levels of data to meet their unique objectives and contribute to the overall success of the company. This table outlines the personas, their roles related to analytics, and the recommended tools to support those functions on high level.

 

PersonaRole Description and ExamplesRecommended Product
Executive LeadershipHigh-level strategic decision-making, financial performance tracking, and overall business health assessment.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Business Data Cloud 
Managers (e.g., Sales, Supply Chain)Sales performance tracking, pipeline management, identifying sales opportunities/inventory management, supplier performance, and logistics optimization.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial AnalystsBudget analysis, investment performance, managing the company's liquidity, funding, capital management, organize and control receivables and collections, etc.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial PlanningProvides overall financial planning and performance management insights.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud e.g. via Rapid FP&A for SAP S/4HANA 

Now, let's go deeper and let me provide you an overview on the analytical tools that SAP offers inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. More information can also be found here.

back to top

Review Booklet

As this is one of the new kids on the blog. I'd like to start with the Review Booklet. In essence, Review Booklets provide pre-configured, user-friendly business pages designed to address common and pertinent business inquiries for your day-to-day tasks right out of the box. They merge application logic and direction with built-in multidimensional analytics, enhanced further by advanced AI capabilities as they evolve. SAP ships already multiple Review Booklets with out-of-the-box content. We also know that our content never hits 100% of your requirements, thus it is possible to adjust the content via the Review Booklet Designer. An image or video says more that thousand words when it comes to data visualization, so please take a look at this example of a Review Booklet:

Key benefits of Review Booklet:

  1. Consolidated Reporting: Gather multiple reports into a single booklet format, streamlining access and enhancing information organization for more efficient review.
  2. Predefined Layouts for Business Queries: Utilize ready-made layouts tailored to address specific business questions, providing immediate insights without extensive customization.
  3. Guided Analysis with Multidimensional Insights: Experience guided and flexible analysis modes enriched by contextual actions and generic pivoting, seamlessly combining application logic with integrated multidimensional insights to support thorough exploration and understanding

back to top

Multidimensional Analysis

The second 'new' kid is the Multidimensional Analysis report. Multidimensional Analysis offers a flexible and interactive way to delve into complex datasets, enabling users to uncover insights more intuitively. It begins with an analytical query in its default view, serving as a starting point for exploration. From there, users can pivot and drill down into the data, adding dimensions, measures, and structures to rows and columns to tailor the analysis to specific needs. This approach is particularly beneficial for advanced users, facilitating deep data exploration and empowering them to discover trends and patterns that drive informed decision-making. For more info, check out this blog. Multidimensional Analysis will replace the reports based on the 'old' technology of Web Dynpro Grid in the future, so better get yourself familiar with it.

Key benefits of Multidimensional Analysis reports:

  1. Flexible and Interactive Exploration: Enjoy adaptability through interactive pivoting and drill-down capabilities, allowing users to dynamically manipulate data for deeper insights.
  2. Customizable Data Views: Begin with a default view of an analytical query and progressively enhance your exploration by adding various dimensions, measures, and structures to both rows and columns for tailored analysis.
  3. Advanced Data Exploration: Cater to advanced users seeking comprehensive understanding, offering powerful tools for extensive data investigation and analysis.

Figure: shows a Multidimensional Analysis on Income Statement and how to add a calculation on the fly.

back to top

Overview Page

An overview page is a unique SAP Fiori app designed to enhance user experience by offering a streamlined structure. Each overview page is composed of predefined cards of varying types displayed on a canvas beneath a filter bar. Adjustments made in the filter bar apply universally to all cards within the app, ensuring cohesiveness in data presentation. Users can interact with the cards by clicking on them, allowing navigation to additional apps such as reports, KPI details, or transactional applications.

Key benefits of Overview Page:

  1. KPI Cards for Immediate Insight: Deliver a quick and comprehensive snapshot of a domain by showcasing key performance measures, enabling users to grasp the overall picture swiftly.
  2. Aggregated Data Display: Present data in a consolidated format, streamlining information to enhance clarity and facilitate informed decision-making.
  3. Navigation Options: Enable deeper investigation through seamless navigation, positioning overview pages as an ideal entry point for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Procurement Overview Page for Purchaser.

back to top

Analytical List Page

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is an integral component of SAP Fiori designed to transform business key performance indicators into dynamic analytical dashboards. These dashboards incorporate KPIs, charts, tables, and visual filters to provide insightful data visualizations. Powered by SAP UI annotations, ALP offers the flexibility to explore data from various angles, allowing for in-depth analysis of root causes and supporting timely decision-making. 

Key Benefits of ALP:

  1. Insight to Action is Key: Empower business users to process their work lists in context with their analytical findings, driving effective outcomes.
  2. Focus on Critical Issues: Easily highlight and address the most pressing concerns, and take immediate action without having to switch screens.
  3. Prioritize Efficiency: Sort and filter work lists to ensure that the most critical issues are prioritized and dealt with promptly.

Figure: Supervise Collection Worklist for Accounts Receivable Manager.

back to top

Smart Business KPIs (including Smart Business Runtime aka generic drill-down)

The KPIs are presented on tile surfaces using either simple numerical values or mini-charts, organized through the SAP Fiori Launchpad. Business users have the flexibility to select which KPIs to display on their personalized Launchpads, choosing from catalogs assigned to their roles. For deeper analysis, users can easily click on a tile to access detailed drill-down information. The Smart Business runtime environment provides access to detailed configurations of evaluations, enabling users to drill down and analyze KPIs in detail. The runtime is enhanced with smart controls, such as smart filters and smart charts, to further streamline the analytical process.

Key benefits of Smart Business KPI reports:

  1. Visual and Color-Coded KPI Representation: Display KPIs the SAP Fiori launchpad with simple numerical values, utilizing colors to offer immediate visual cues and enhance comprehension of performance levels.
  2. Instant Performance Overview: Provide an at-a-glance view of business performance, enabling quick assessments and timely decision-making.

Figure: Analysis section on the Fiori Launchpad for Accounts Receivables Managers opening the Smart Business generic drill-down app Overdue Receivables.

back to top

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story

The SAP Analytics Cloud embedded version integrates seamlessly with the SAP Fiori UI of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the so called Stories offer thereby an additional tool to analyze business data. Stories are designed to transform raw data into insightful visualizations, empowering users to enhance business performance and make informed, data-driven decisions including seamless navigation to and from other S/4HANA Cloud apps. These stories reveal hidden insights in your data by allowing the addition of text, images, and generating dynamic visualizations such as charts and graphs. Of course, SAP offers a large set of out-of-the-box content which can serve as a quick start and can be customized according to the requirements. 

Key benefits of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story:

  1. Powerful Visualization and Feature-Rich Dashboards: Create dynamic and visually compelling analytical dashboards that enhance understanding and decision-making.
  2. Interactive Data Exploration: Easily explore data through linked analysis, page filters, chart filters, and input controls, providing a user-friendly experience for in-depth insights.
  3. Advanced Data Analysis: Utilize location analytics, perform calculations on the fly, and analyze multiple queries for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Purchasing Spend Dashboard for Purchasers.

back to top

SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel

There is also an option to analyze business data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel. However, this reporting tool is not embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but can be opened via an app from SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Figure: Opening SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel from Fiori Launchpad. 

back to top

Checklist

Now consolidating all the information above, I come to the following checklists that shall help preselecting possible tool candidates by ticking the checkboxes according to your needs:

back to top

Feature / Function / Capabilities focus

Now that I provided an holistic overview on all the available options to analyze your business data, I'd like to get into the details. Important to know is that the following tables show the strengths of each tool, there is no one fits all solution as different information requirements and perspectives require different capabilities. 

I will explain the one or the other capability to make sure that you understand these tables correctly. Please leave a comment if something is not clear.

Legend 

++

Yes, supported and additional features/functions available

+

Yes, supported but limited (explanation in brackets)

-

Not supported

 

Visualization Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis
